 52→public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 53→    StandardRule(
 54→        id = "function-signature",
 55→        visitorModifiers =
 56→            setOf(
 57→                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 58→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 59→            ),
 60→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 61→            setOf(
 62→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 63→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 64→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 65→                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 66→                FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 67→            ),
 68→    ),
 69→    Rule.Experimental {
 70→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 71→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 72→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 73→    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 74→        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 75→    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76→
 77→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 78→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 79→        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 80→        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 81→        indentConfig =
 82→            IndentConfig(
 83→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 84→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 85→            )
 86→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90→        node: ASTNode,
 91→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93→    ) {
 94→        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 95→            node
 96→                .functionSignatureNodes()
 97→                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 98→                .ifTrue {
 99→                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
100→                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
101→                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
102→                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
103→                    // effort to attempt it.
104→                    return
105→                }
106→
107→            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
108→        }
109→    }
110→
111→    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
112→        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
113→        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
114→        // or
115→        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
116→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
117→        val startOfBodyBlock =
118→            this
119→                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
120→                ?.firstChildNode
121→        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
122→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
123→            .childrenBetween(
124→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
125→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
126→            )
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
130→        val funNode =
131→            if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
132→                this.treeParent
133→            } else {
134→                this
135→            }
136→        funNode
137→            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
138→            ?.let { modifierList ->
139→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
140→                var currentNode: ASTNode
141→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
142→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
143→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
144→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
145→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
146→                    ) {
147→                        return currentNode
148→                    }
149→                }
150→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
151→            }
152→        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
153→    }
154→
155→    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
156→        node: ASTNode,
157→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
158→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
159→    ) {
160→        require(node.elementType == FUN)
161→
162→        val forceMultilineSignature =
163→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
164→                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
165→                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
166→        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
167→            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
168→            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
169→            // like below, which are not acceptable:
170→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
171→            //     ) = "some-value"
172→            //
173→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
174→            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
175→            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
176→            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
177→            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
178→            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
179→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
180→                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
181→                // signature needs to be recalculated
182→                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
183→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
184→            } else {
185→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
186→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
187→            }
188→        } else {
189→            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
190→            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
191→            // signature.
192→            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
193→                node
194→                    .functionSignatureNodes()
195→                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
196→            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
197→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
198→            } else {
199→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
200→            }
201→        }
202→    }
203→
204→    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
205→        val closingParenthesis =
206→            node
207→                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
208→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
209→        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
210→            node
211→                .functionSignatureNodes()
212→                .childrenBetween(
213→                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
214→                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
215→                )
216→
217→        return node.indent(false).length +
218→            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
219→    }
220→
221→    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
222→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
223→            ?.children()
224→            .orEmpty()
225→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
226→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
227→
228→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
229→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
230→            ?.children()
231→            .orEmpty()
232→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
233→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
234→
235→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
236→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
237→            ?.children()
238→            .orEmpty()
239→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
240→
241→    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
242→        node: ASTNode,
243→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→    ): Int {
246→        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
247→
248→        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
249→        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
250→        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
251→            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
252→            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
253→    }
254→
255→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
258→        functionSignatureNodes()
259→            .joinTextToString()
260→            .length
261→
262→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
263→        node: ASTNode,
264→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
265→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
266→        multiline: Boolean,
267→        dryRun: Boolean,
268→    ): Int {
269→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
270→
271→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
272→        val firstParameterInList =
273→            valueParameterList
274→                .children()
275→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
276→
277→        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
278→            if (firstParameterInList == null) {
279→                // handle empty parameter list
280→                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
281→            } else {
282→                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
283→                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
284→                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
285→            }
286→
287→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
288→    }
289→
290→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
291→        node: ASTNode,
292→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
293→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
294→        dryRun: Boolean,
295→    ): Int {
296→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
297→
298→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
299→
300→        valueParameterList
301→            .children()
302→            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
303→            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
304→                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
305→                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
306→                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
307→            }
308→            .firstOrNull()
309→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
310→                if (!dryRun) {
311→                    emit(
312→                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
313→                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
314→                        true,
315→                    )
316→                }
317→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
318→                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
319→                } else {
320→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
321→                }
322→            }
323→
324→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
325→    }
326→
327→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
328→        node: ASTNode,
329→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
330→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
331→        multiline: Boolean,
332→        dryRun: Boolean,
333→    ): Int {
334→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
335→
336→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
337→        val firstParameterInList =
338→            valueParameterList
339→                .children()
340→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
341→
342→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
343→        firstParameter
344→            ?.prevLeaf()
345→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
346→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
347→                if (multiline) {
348→                    val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
349→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
350→                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
351→                    ) {
352→                        if (!dryRun) {
353→                            emit(
354→                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
355→                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
356→                                true,
357→                            )
358→                        }
359→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361→                        } else {
362→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363→                        }
364→                    }
365→                } else {
366→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
367→                        if (!dryRun) {
368→                            emit(
369→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
370→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
371→                                true,
372→                            )
373→                        }
374→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
375→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
376→                        } else {
377→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
378→                        }
379→                    }
380→                }
381→            }
382→
383→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
384→    }
385→
386→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
387→        node: ASTNode,
388→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
389→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
390→        multiline: Boolean,
391→        dryRun: Boolean,
392→    ): Int {
393→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
394→
395→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
396→        val firstParameterInList =
397→            valueParameterList
398→                .children()
399→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
400→
401→        valueParameterList
402→            .children()
403→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
404→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
405→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
406→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
407→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
408→                    ?.prevLeaf()
409→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
410→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
411→                        if (multiline) {
412→                            val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
413→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
414→                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
415→                            ) {
416→                                if (!dryRun) {
417→                                    emit(
418→                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
419→                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
420→                                        true,
421→                                    )
422→                                }
423→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
424→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
425→                                } else {
426→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
427→                                }
428→                            }
429→                        } else {
430→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
431→                                if (!dryRun) {
432→                                    emit(
433→                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
434→                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
435→                                        true,
436→                                    )
437→                                }
438→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
439→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
440→                                } else {
441→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
442→                                }
443→                            }
444→                        }
445→                    }
446→            }
447→
448→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
449→    }
450→
451→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
452→        node: ASTNode,
453→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
454→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
455→        multiline: Boolean,
456→        dryRun: Boolean,
457→    ): Int {
458→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
459→
460→        val newlineAndIndent = node.indent()
461→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
462→
463→        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
464→        closingParenthesis
465→            ?.prevSibling()
466→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
467→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
468→                if (multiline) {
469→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
470→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
471→                    ) {
472→                        if (!dryRun) {
473→                            emit(
474→                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
475→                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
476→                                true,
477→                            )
478→                        }
479→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
480→                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
481→                        } else {
482→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
483→                        }
484→                    }
485→                } else {
486→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
487→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
488→                    ) {
489→                        if (!dryRun) {
490→                            emit(
491→                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
492→                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
493→                                true,
494→                            )
495→                        }
496→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
497→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
498→                        } else {
499→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
500→                        }
501→                    }
502→                }
503→            }
504→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
505→    }
506→
507→    private fun fixFunctionBody(
508→        node: ASTNode,
509→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
510→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
511→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
512→    ) {
513→        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
514→            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
515→        } else {
516→            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)
517→        }
518→    }
519→
520→    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
521→        node: ASTNode,
522→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
523→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
524→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
525→    ) {
526→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
527→            node
528→                .findChildByType(EQ)
529→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
530→                ?: return
531→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
532→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
533→        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
534→
535→        val functionBodyExpressionLines =
536→            functionBodyExpressionNodes
537→                .joinTextToString()
538→                .split("\n")
539→        functionBodyExpressionLines
540→            .firstOrNull()
541→            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
542→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
543→                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
544→                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
545→                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
546→                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
547→                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
548→                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
549→                                )
550→                        } else {
551→                            false
552→                        }
553→                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
554→                        emit(
555→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
556→                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
557→                            true,
558→                        )
559→                        if (autoCorrect) {
560→                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
561→                        }
562→                    }
563→                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
564→                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
565→                ) {
566→                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
567→                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
568→                    ) {
569→                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
570→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
571→                        ) {
572→                            emit(
573→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
574→                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
575→                                true,
576→                            )
577→                            if (autoCorrect) {
578→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
579→                                    .first()
580→                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
581→                            }
582→                        }
583→                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
584→                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
585→                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
586→                    ) {
587→                        emit(
588→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
589→                            "Newline expected before expression body",
590→                            true,
591→                        )
592→                        if (autoCorrect) {
593→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
594→                                .first()
595→                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
596→                        }
597→                    }
598→                }
599→            }
600→    }
601→
602→    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
603→        functionSignatureNodes()
604→            .childrenBetween(
605→                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
606→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
607→            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
608→            .split("\n")
609→            .lastOrNull()
610→            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
611→            ?: false
612→
613→    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
614→        node: ASTNode,
615→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
616→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
617→    ) {
618→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
619→            node
620→                .getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody()
621→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
622→                ?: return
623→
624→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
625→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
626→        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
627→
628→        functionBodyBlock
629→            .joinTextToString()
630→            .split("\n")
631→            .firstOrNull()
632→            ?.also {
633→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
634→                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
635→                    if (autoCorrect) {
636→                        functionBodyBlock
637→                            .first()
638→                            .prevLeaf(true)
639→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
640→                    }
641→                }
642→            }
643→    }
644→
645→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionBody(splitNode: ASTNode?): List<ASTNode> =
646→        this
647→            .collectLeavesRecursively()
648→            .childrenBetween(
649→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == splitNode },
650→                endASTNodePredicate = {
651→                    // collect all remaining nodes
652→                    false
653→                },
654→            )
655→
656→    private fun List<ASTNode>.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull() =
657→        this
658→            .firstOrNull()
659→            ?.takeIf { first -> first.isWhiteSpace() }
660→
661→    private fun isMaxLineLengthSet() = maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
662→
663→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
664→
665→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
666→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
667→            listOf(this)
668→        } else {
669→            children()
670→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
671→                .toList()
672→        }
673→
674→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
675→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
676→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
677→    ): List<ASTNode> {
678→        val iterator = iterator()
679→        var currentNode: ASTNode
680→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
681→
682→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
683→            currentNode = iterator.next()
684→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
685→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
686→                break
687→            }
688→        }
689→
690→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
691→            currentNode = iterator.next()
692→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
693→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
694→                break
695→            }
696→        }
697→
698→        return childrenBetween
699→    }
700→
701→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
702→        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }
703→
704→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
705→
706→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {
707→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
708→        return valueParameterList
709→            .children()
710→            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
711→    }
712→
713→    private fun ASTNode.getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody(): ASTNode? =
714→        this
715→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
716→            ?.firstChildNode
717→            ?.prevCodeLeaf()
718→
719→    public companion object {
720→        private const val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET = Int.MAX_VALUE
721→        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
722→            EditorConfigProperty(
723→                type =
724→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
725→                        "ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
726→                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the function signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
727→                            "number of parameters even in case the entire function signature would fit on a single line. " +
728→                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
729→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
730→                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
731→                    ),
732→                defaultValue = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET,
733→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 2,
734→                propertyMapper = { property, _ ->
735→                    if (property?.isUnset == true) {
736→                        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET
737→                    } else {
738→                        property?.getValueAs<Int>()
739→                    }
740→                },
741→                propertyWriter = { property ->
742→                    if (property == FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET) {
743→                        "unset"
744→                    } else {
745→                        property.toString()
746→                    }
747→                },
748→            )
749→
750→        public val FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping> =
751→            EditorConfigProperty(
752→                type =
753→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
754→                        "ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping",
755→                        "Determines how to wrap the body of function in case it is an expression. Use 'default' " +
756→                            "to wrap the body expression only when the first line of the expression does not fit on the same " +
757→                            "line as the function signature. Use 'multiline' to force wrapping of body expressions that " +
758→                            "consists of multiple line. Use 'always' to force wrapping of body expression always.",
759→                        EnumValueParser(FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping::class.java),
760→                        FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
761→                    ),
762→                defaultValue = default,
763→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = multiline,
764→            )
765→
766→        private val INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS = Regex("\\s*\\) =")
767→    }
768→
769→    /**
770→     * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
771→     */
772→    @Suppress("EnumEntryName")
773→    public enum class FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping {
774→        /**
775→         * Keep the first line of the body expression on the same line as the function signature if max line length is
776→         * not exceeded.
777→         */
778→        default,
779→
780→        /**
781→         * Force the body expression to start on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line
782→         * body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
783→         */
784→        multiline,
785→
786→        /**
787→         * Always force the body expression to start on a separate line.
788→         */
789→        always,
790→    }
791→}
792→
793→public val FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionSignatureRule().ruleId

content.text:
I have analyzed the issue where lambda parameters were incorrectly indented or reported with incorrect indentation when wrapped, especially in non-`ktlint_official` code styles.

The root cause of the issue was two-fold:
1.  **Context removal timing:** The `IndentationRule` started an indent context for lambda parameters that ended at the last leaf before the arrow (`arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!`). In some cases, especially when parameters were on new lines, this context could be removed from the stack too early, causing subsequent elements or whitespaces on the same line to be measured against the outer lambda body context instead of the specific parameter context.
2.  **Robustness of callee detection:** The calculation of the expected indentation for parameters (intended to align with the opening brace of the lambda in `intellij_idea` style) relied on finding a `REFERENCE_EXPRESSION` as a direct child of the `CALL_EXPRESSION`. If the callee was more complex or the PSI structure differed slightly, this could fail and fall back to a default double-indent, which might not match the user's expectations or the actual code.

I have implemented the following fixes in `IndentationRule.kt`:
-   Modified `visitLbrace` to extend the parameter indent context until the `ARROW` node itself. This ensures the context remains active for all parameters and any associated whitespace until the arrow is reached.
-   Improved `calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters` to more robustly detect the callee's text. It now falls back to the first child node of the `CALL_EXPRESSION` if a direct `REFERENCE_EXPRESSION` is not found, and it handles potential null values safely to avoid incorrect string concatenation (like `"null { "`).

These changes ensure that wrapped lambda parameters are correctly indented and that `ktlint` accurately calculates and reports their expected indentation, maintaining compatibility with IntelliJ IDEA formatting while being more robust.

I have verified the fix with reproduction test cases covering both `intellij_idea` and `ktlint_official` code styles with various indentation settings.

 242→                visitIf(node)
 243→
 244→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 245→                visitLbrace(node)
 246→
 247→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 248→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 249→                startIndentContext(
 250→                    fromAstNode = node,
 251→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 252→                )
 253→
 254→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 255→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 256→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 259→                visitValueParameter(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 262→                visitFun(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 265→                visitClass(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 268→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 269→
 270→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 271→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 272→
 273→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 274→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 275→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 276→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 277→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 278→                ) {
 279→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 280→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 281→                    //    .bar[0] {
 282→                    //        "foobar"
 283→                    //    }
 284→                    startIndentContext(
 285→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 286→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 287→                    )
 288→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 289→                    startIndentContext(node)
 290→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 291→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 292→                } else {
 293→                    startIndentContext(node)
 294→                }
 295→            }
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 298→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 299→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 302→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 303→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 304→
 305→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 306→                visitWhen(node)
 307→
 308→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 309→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 310→
 311→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 312→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 313→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 314→
 315→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 316→                visitKdoc(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 319→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 320→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 321→
 322→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 323→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 324→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 325→
 326→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 327→                visitLBracket(node)
 328→
 329→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 330→                visitNullableType(node)
 331→
 332→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 333→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 334→
 335→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 336→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 337→
 338→            else -> {
 339→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 340→            }
 341→        }
 342→    }
 343→
 344→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 345→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 346→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 347→            //     val foo = foo(
 348→            //         parameterName =
 349→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 350→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 351→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 352→            //     )
 353→            startIndentContext(
 354→                fromAstNode = node,
 355→                lastChildIndent = "",
 356→            )
 357→        }
 358→    }
 359→
 360→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 361→        node
 362→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 363→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 364→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 365→                val nextToAstNode =
 366→                    startIndentContext(
 367→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 368→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369→
 370→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 371→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 372→                    startIndentContext(
 373→                        fromAstNode = node,
 374→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 375→                        childIndent = "",
 376→                    )
 377→                }
 378→            }
 379→    }
 380→
 381→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 382→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 383→        node
 384→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 385→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 386→                nextToAstNode =
 387→                    startIndentContext(
 388→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 389→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 390→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 391→            }
 392→
 393→        node
 394→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 395→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 396→            ?.nextLeaf()
 397→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 398→                nextToAstNode =
 399→                    startIndentContext(
 400→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 401→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                        childIndent = "",
 403→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 404→            }
 405→        node
 406→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 407→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 408→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 409→                nextToAstNode =
 410→                    startIndentContext(
 411→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 412→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 413→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 414→            }
 415→        startIndentContext(
 416→            fromAstNode = node,
 417→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 418→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 419→        )
 420→    }
 421→
 422→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 423→        // Outer indent context
 424→        val rbrace =
 425→            requireNotNull(
 426→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 427→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 428→        startIndentContext(
 429→            fromAstNode = node,
 430→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 431→            firstChildIndent = "",
 432→            lastChildIndent = "",
 433→        )
 434→
 435→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 436→        node
 437→            .treeParent
 438→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 439→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 440→            ?.let { arrow ->
 441→                startIndentContext(
 442→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 443→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 444→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 445→                )
 446→                startIndentContext(
 447→                    fromAstNode = node,
 448→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 449→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 450→                )
 451→            }
 452→    }
 453→
 454→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 455→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 456→            // val fieldExample =
 457→            //      LongNameClass {
 458→            //              paramA,
 459→            //              paramB,
 460→            //              paramC ->
 461→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 462→            //      }
 463→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 464→        } else {
 465→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 466→            // val fieldExample =
 467→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 468→            //                     paramB,
 469→            //                     paramC ->
 470→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 471→            //     }
 472→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 473→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 474→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 475→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 476→                            " { "
 477→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 478→                }
 479→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 480→        }
 481→
 482→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 483→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 484→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 485→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 486→
 487→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 488→        startIndentContext(
 489→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 490→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 491→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 492→            childIndent = "",
 493→        )
 494→    }
 495→
 496→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 497→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 498→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 499→        node
 500→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 501→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 502→                nextToAstNode =
 503→                    startIndentContext(
 504→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 505→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 506→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 507→            }
 508→
 509→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 510→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 511→            //     fun process(
 512→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 513→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 514→            //     ): List<Output>
 515→            node
 516→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 517→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 518→                    nextToAstNode =
 519→                        startIndentContext(
 520→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 521→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 522→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 523→                }
 524→        }
 525→
 526→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 527→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 528→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 529→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 530→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 531→        ) {
 532→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 533→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 534→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 535→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 536→        } else {
 537→            startIndentContext(
 538→                fromAstNode = node,
 539→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 540→                childIndent = "",
 541→            )
 542→        }
 543→    }
 544→
 545→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 546→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 547→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 548→        val eqOrBlock =
 549→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 550→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 551→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 552→            nextToAstNode =
 553→                startIndentContext(
 554→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 555→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 556→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 557→        }
 558→
 559→        node
 560→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 561→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 562→                nextToAstNode =
 563→                    startIndentContext(
 564→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 565→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 566→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 567→            }
 568→
 569→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 570→        startIndentContext(
 571→            fromAstNode = node,
 572→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 573→            childIndent = "",
 574→        )
 575→    }
 576→
 577→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 578→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 579→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 580→        node
 581→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 582→            ?.let { where ->
 583→                val typeConstraintList =
 584→                    requireNotNull(
 585→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 586→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 587→                require(
 588→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 589→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 590→                nextToAstNode =
 591→                    startIndentContext(
 592→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 593→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 594→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 595→            }
 596→
 597→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 598→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
 599→            // Indent both constructor and super type list
 600→            nextToAstNode =
 601→                startIndentContext(
 602→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 603→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 604→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 605→        } else {
 606→            node
 607→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 608→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 609→                    nextToAstNode =
 610→                        startIndentContext(
 611→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 612→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 613→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 614→                }
 615→        }
 616→
 617→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 618→        startIndentContext(
 619→            fromAstNode = node,
 620→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 621→            childIndent = "",
 622→        )
 623→    }
 624→
 625→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 626→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 627→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 628→        node
 629→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 630→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 631→                nextToAstNode =
 632→                    startIndentContext(
 633→                        fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 634→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 635→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 636→            }
 637→
 638→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 639→        startIndentContext(
 640→            fromAstNode = node,
 641→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 642→            childIndent = "",
 643→        )
 644→    }

 646→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 647→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 648→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 649→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 650→            val conditionIndentContext =
 651→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 652→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 653→            startIndentContext(
 654→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 655→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 656→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 657→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 658→            )
 659→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 660→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 661→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 662→        ) {
 663→            startIndentContext(node)
 664→        }
 665→    }
 666→
 667→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 668→        startIndentContext(
 669→            fromAstNode = node,
 670→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 671→        )
 672→    }
 673→
 674→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 675→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 676→        node
 677→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 678→            ?.let { lpar ->
 679→                nextToASTNode =
 680→                    startIndentContext(
 681→                        fromAstNode = lpar,
 682→                        toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 683→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 684→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 685→            }
 686→        startIndentContext(
 687→            fromAstNode = node,
 688→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 689→        )
 690→    }
 691→
 692→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 693→        node
 694→            .findChildByType(ARROW)
 695→            ?.let { arrow ->
 696→                val outerIndent =
 697→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 698→                        ""
 699→                    } else {
 700→                        indentConfig.indent
 701→                    }
 702→                startIndentContext(
 703→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 704→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 705→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 706→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 707→                )
 708→                startIndentContext(
 709→                    fromAstNode = node,
 710→                    toAstNode = arrow,
 711→                    childIndent = "",
 712→                )
 713→            }
 714→    }

 716→    private fun visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node: ASTNode) {
 717→        startIndentContext(
 718→            fromAstNode = node,
 719→            toAstNode = node.nextCodeSibling()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()!!,
 720→            childIndent = TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 721→        )
 722→    }
 723→
 724→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {
 725→        node
 726→            .findChildByType(KDOC_START)
 727→            ?.nextLeaf()
 728→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 729→                startIndentContext(
 730→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 731→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 732→                    childIndent = KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 733→                )
 734→            }
 735→    }

 737→    private fun visitPropertyAccessor(node: ASTNode) {
 738→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 739→        node
 740→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 741→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 742→                nextToASTNode =
 743→                    startIndentContext(
 744→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 745→                        toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 746→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 747→            }
 748→        // No indent on preceding annotations and comments
 749→        startIndentContext(
 750→            fromAstNode = node,
 751→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,

 753→        )

 756→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {
 757→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 758→        node
 759→            .findChildByType(BODY)
 760→            ?.takeIf { it.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType != LBRACE }
 761→            ?.let { rpar ->
 762→                startIndentContext(
 763→                    fromAstNode = rpar,
 764→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 765→                )
 766→            }
 767→        node
 768→            .findChildByType(CONDITION)
 769→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 770→                startIndentContext(fromAstNode)
 771→            }
 772→    }
 773→
 774→    private fun visitLBracket(node: ASTNode) {
 775→        node
 776→            .treeParent
 777→            .takeUnless {
 778→                // Should be resolved in IntelliJ IDEA default formatter:
 779→                // https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-14859/Too-little-indentation-inside-the-brackets-in-multiple-annotations-with-the-same-target
 780→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION
 781→            }?.findChildByType(RBRACKET)
 782→            ?.let { rbracket ->
 783→                startIndentContext(
 784→                    fromAstNode = node,
 785→                    toAstNode = rbracket,
 786→                    firstChildIndent = "",
 787→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 788→                )
 789→            }
 790→    }

 792→    private fun visitNullableType(node: ASTNode) {
 793→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 794→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 795→        node
 796→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 797→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 798→                nextToAstNode =
 799→                    startIndentContext(
 800→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 801→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 802→                        childIndent = "",
 803→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 804→            }
 805→        startIndentContext(
 806→            fromAstNode = node,
 807→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 808→        )
 809→    }

 811→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 812→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 813→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 814→        node
 815→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 816→            ?.let { eq ->
 817→                nextToAstNode =
 818→                    startIndentContext(
 819→                        fromAstNode = eq,
 820→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 821→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 822→            }
 823→
 824→        node
 825→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 826→            ?.let {
 827→                startIndentContext(
 828→                    fromAstNode = node,
 829→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 830→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 831→                )
 832→            }
 833→    }
 834→
 835→    private fun visitTryCatchFinally(node: ASTNode) {
 836→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 837→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 838→        node
 839→            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
 840→            ?.let { finally ->
 841→                nextToAstNode =
 842→                    startIndentContext(
 843→                        fromAstNode = finally,
 844→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 845→                        childIndent = "",
 846→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 847→            }
 848→
 849→        node
 850→            .findChildByType(CATCH)
 851→            ?.let { catch ->
 852→                nextToAstNode =
 853→                    startIndentContext(
 854→                        fromAstNode = catch,
 855→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 856→                        childIndent = "",
 857→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 858→            }
 859→
 860→        startIndentContext(
 861→            fromAstNode = node,
 862→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 863→            lastChildIndent = "",
 864→        )
 865→    }
 866→
 867→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 868→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 869→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 870→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 871→                modifierList
 872→                    .children()
 873→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 874→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 875→            }
 876→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 877→            ?: this
 878→    }
 879→
 880→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 881→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 882→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 883→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 884→        ) {
 885→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 886→        }
 887→        return fromAstNode!!
 888→    }
 889→
 890→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 891→        node
 892→            .parents()
 893→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 894→            .lastOrNull()
 895→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 896→
 897→    private fun currentIndent() =
 898→        indentContextStack
 899→            .peekLast()
 900→            .indent()
 901→
 902→    private fun startIndentContext(
 903→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 904→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),

 920→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)

 928→        node: ASTNode,

 930→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 931→    ) {

 934→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()

 938→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"

 941→                .removeLast()

 961→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 962→        node: ASTNode,
 963→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 964→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 965→    ) {
 966→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 967→            return
 968→        }
 969→
 970→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 971→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 972→        val text = node.text
 973→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 974→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 975→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 976→                emit(
 977→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 978→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 979→                    true,
 980→                )
 981→            } else {
 982→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 983→                // been emitted.
 984→            }
 985→            LOGGER.trace {
 986→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 987→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 988→            }
 989→            if (autoCorrect) {
 990→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 991→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 992→                )
 993→            }
 994→        } else {
 995→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 996→        }
 997→    }

 999→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1000→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1001→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1002→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1003→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1004→        }
1005→
1006→        nextLeaf
1007→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1008→            ?.let { comment ->
1009→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1010→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1011→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1012→                }
1013→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1014→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1015→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1016→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1017→                    return true
1018→                }
1019→            }
1020→        return false
1021→    }

1025→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =

1028→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1029→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1030→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1031→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1032→            when {
1033→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1034→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1035→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1036→
1037→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1038→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1039→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1040→
1041→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1042→            }
1043→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1044→    }

1046→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1047→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1048→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1049→            SPACE -> {
1050→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1051→                    emit(
1052→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1053→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1054→                        true,
1055→                    )
1056→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1057→                } else {
1058→                    nodeIndent
1059→                }
1060→            }
1061→
1062→            TAB -> {
1063→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1064→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1065→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1066→                    emit(
1067→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1068→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1069→                        true,
1070→                    )
1071→                    indentConfig
1072→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1073→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1074→                } else {
1075→                    nodeIndent
1076→                }
1077→            }
1078→        }
1079→    }

1081→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1082→        node: ASTNode,
1083→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1084→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1085→    ) {
1086→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1087→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(codeStyle, indentConfig)

1089→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)

1097→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1098→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1099→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1100→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1101→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1102→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1103→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1104→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1105→                }
1106→
1107→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1108→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1109→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1110→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1111→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1112→                }
1113→
1114→                else -> ""
1115→            }
1116→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1117→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1118→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1119→        } else {
1120→            ""
1121→        }
1122→    }

1124→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1125→        IndentContext(
1126→            fromASTNode = node,
1127→            nodeIndent = "",
1128→            firstChildIndent = "",
1129→            childIndent = "",
1130→            lastChildIndent = "",
1131→            activated = true,
1132→        )

1134→    private companion object {
1135→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1136→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1137→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)

1140→    private data class IndentContext(
1141→        /**
1142→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1143→         */
1144→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1145→        /**
1146→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1147→         * on which the indent context starts.
1148→         */
1149→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1150→        /**
1151→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1152→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1153→         */
1154→        val nodeIndent: String,
1155→        /**
1156→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1157→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1158→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1159→         */
1160→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1161→        /**
1162→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1163→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1164→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1165→         */
1166→        val childIndent: String,
1167→        /**
1168→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1169→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1170→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1171→         */
1172→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1173→        /**
1174→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1175→         */
1176→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1177→    ) {
1178→        val nodes: String
1179→            get() =
1180→                fromASTNode
1181→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1182→                    ?.leaves()
1183→                    .orEmpty()
1184→                    .takeWhile {
1185→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1186→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1187→                    }
1188→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1189→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1190→
1191→        fun indent() =
1192→            if (activated) {
1193→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1194→            } else {
1195→                nodeIndent
1196→            }
1197→
1198→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1199→    }
1200→}

1202→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()

1204→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1205→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1206→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1207→            Pair("[", "]")
1208→        } else {
1209→            Pair("", "")
1210→        }
1211→    return prefix
1212→        .plus(
1213→            this
1214→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1215→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1216→        ).plus(suffix)
1217→}
